transcript.content: Can you publish the churn cohort update to growth-review? The dashboard already has a number, but use the warehouse if that is safer.

args.query: SELECT state, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(metric_value) AS total, AVG(metric_value) AS avg_val, SUM(CASE WHEN reconciled THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS reconciled_count, MIN(loaded_at) AS min_loaded, MAX(loaded_at) AS max_loaded FROM churn_events GROUP BY state ORDER BY n DESC;

args.title: Churn Cohort Update
args.body:
Source: churn_events warehouse table (preferred over dashboard cache).

Headline: Reconciled churn cohort metric = 13 (2 finalized events, loaded 2026-04-30).

Detail:
- Final / reconciled events: 2 rows, metric_value sum = 13 (7 + 6), loaded_at 2026-04-30.
- Cached / unreconciled row: 1 row, metric_value = 10, loaded_at 2026-04-29. This is the value currently reflected on the dashboard and is stale — it predates reconciliation.

Recommendation: Use 13 (reconciled) as the cohort number for growth review. The dashboard's 10 should be refreshed; it is showing the pre-reconciliation cached snapshot.
